perturbation.original_text: Each party will disclose such information solely for the purpose of evaluating the possibility of entering into a business relationship between the parties hereto (the “Purpose”).
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will disclose such information for the purpose of evaluating the possibility of entering into a business relationship between the parties hereto (the “Purpose”), but may also use the disclosed information for any other purpose they deem necessary.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the use of disclosed information to evaluating a business relationship. The modified text introduces a contradictory clause allowing use 'for any other purpose they deem necessary'. This undermines the confidentiality implied by the agreement's title and the original restrictive clause. This introduces an in-text contradiction, where the purpose can be any purpose, and also evaluating a business relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall: (a) only disclose the Confidential Information to those employees and contractors with a need to know; provided that Recipient binds those employees and contractors to terms at least as restrictive as those stated in this Agreement (or, with respect to professional advisors, advises them of its obligations with respect to the Confidential Information); (b) not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, without Discloser’s prior written consent; (c) use such Confidential Information only to the extent required to accomplish the Purpose; (d) not reproduce Confidential Information in any form except as required to accomplish the Purpose; (e) not reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any software disclosed by the Discloser; (f) not directly or indirectly export or transmit any Confidential Information to any country to which such export or transmission is restricted by regulation or statute; and (g) promptly provide the Discloser with notice of any actual or threatened breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts information use to evaluating a business relationship ('the Purpose'). The changed text allows information use for 'any other purpose they deem necessary', directly contradicting the original restriction. The contradicted text requires the Recipient to 'use such Confidential Information only to the extent required to accomplish the Purpose'. By allowing information use for any purpose, the changed text fundamentally undermines and contradicts the limited usage defined in the contradicted text and the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Definition. Subject to the limitations set forth in Section 2, all information (including but not limited to ideas, techniques, works of authorship, models, inventions, know-how, processes, equipment, algorithms, software source documents and formulae related to the current, future, and proposed products and services of each of the parties and information concerning research, development, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, customer lists, business forecasts, sales and merchandising, and marketing plans), disclosed by one party (the “Discloser”) to the other party (the “Recipient”) which is labeled or marked “Confidential” or with some other similar proprietary legend, or which, in the context of the disclosure, the Recipient has reason to believe is confidential, shall be deemed to be “Confidential Information.”
perturbation.changed_text: Definition. Subject to the limitations set forth in Section 2, only information (including but not limited to ideas, techniques, works of authorship, models, inventions, know-how, processes, equipment, algorithms, software source documents and formulae related to the current, future, and proposed products and services of each of the parties and information concerning research, development, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, customer lists, business forecasts, sales and merchandising, and marketing plans), disclosed by one party (the “Discloser”) to the other party (the “Recipient”) which is labeled or marked “Confidential” or with some other similar proprietary legend, or which, in the context of the disclosure, the Recipient has reason to believe is confidential, shall be deemed to be “Confidential Information.”
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'all information' to 'only information' in the definition of Confidential Information, it creates ambiguity. The original broad definition is now limited. This in-text contradiction impacts Section 4, which governs the 'Ownership and Return of Confidential Information,' because it's unclear what constitutes 'Confidential Information' that must be returned or destroyed. This creates an in-text contradiction of what is considered confidential, based on the use of *only*.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership and Return of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) shall remain the property of Discloser. All copies shall bear all proprietary markings contained on or in the originals. Upon the request of Discloser, Recipient shall, at Recipient’s option, either (a) return such materials to Discloser, or (b) certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'all information' to 'only information' in the definition of 'Confidential Information' creates a direct contradiction with the 'Ownership and Return of Confidential Information' section. The contradicted section refers to 'All Confidential Information', but the modified definition now restricts what qualifies as 'Confidential Information'. This makes it unclear which information is subject to the return or destruction obligations, creating an in-text contradiction as it limits the scope of what must be returned based on the inclusion of *only*.

perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” excludes information which: (a) is now or hereafter becomes generally known or available, through no act or failure to act on Recipient’s part; (b) Recipient independently knows at the time of receiving such information; (c) a third party hereafter furnishes to Recipient without restriction on disclosure and without breach of any confidentiality obligations; (d) Recipient has independently developed without using any Confidential Information or breaching this Agreement; or (e) Discloser gives written permission to Recipient to disclose.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” excludes information which: (a) is now or hereafter becomes generally known or available, through no act or failure to act on Recipient’s part; (b) Recipient independently knows at the time of receiving such information; (c) a third party hereafter furnishes to Recipient with restriction on disclosure; (d) Recipient has independently developed without using any Confidential Information or breaching this Agreement; or (e) Discloser gives written permission to Recipient to Recipient to disclose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without breach of any confidentiality obligations' from exclusion (c), the modified text now implies that information received from a third party *with* confidentiality restrictions is still excluded from the definition of 'Confidential Information' under this agreement. This creates a direct contradiction with the entire purpose of the agreement, which is to protect confidential information. The change introduces an in-text contradiction where information from a third party IS protected even WITH restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall: (a) only disclose the Confidential Information to those employees and contractors with a need to know; provided that Recipient binds those employees and contractors to terms at least as restrictive as those stated in this Agreement (or, with respect to professional advisors, advises them of its obligations with respect to the Confidential Information); (b) not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, without Discloser’s prior written consent; (c) use such Confidential Information only to the extent required to accomplish the Purpose; (d) not reproduce Confidential Information in any form except as required to accomplish the Purpose; (e) not reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any software disclosed by the Discloser; (f) not directly or indirectly export or transmit any Confidential Information to any country to which such export or transmission is restricted by regulation or statute; and (g) promptly provide the Discloser with notice of any actual or threatened breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text excluded information furnished by a third party without restriction on disclosure AND without breach of any confidentiality obligations. The changed text excludes information furnished by a third party WITH restriction on disclosure. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates the Recipient not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, without Discloser’s prior written consent. The change creates a scenario where restricted information from a third party is no longer treated as confidential, despite the Recipient's obligation to protect it.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall: (a) only disclose the Confidential Information to those employees and contractors with a need to know; provided that Recipient binds those employees and contractors to terms at least as restrictive as those stated in this Agreement (or, with respect to professional advisors, advises them of its obligations with respect to the Confidential Information); (b) not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, without Discloser’s prior written consent; (c) use such Confidential Information only to the extent required to accomplish the Purpose; (d) not reproduce Confidential Information in any form except as required to accomplish the Purpose; (e) not reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any software disclosed by the Discloser; (f) not directly or indirectly export or transmit any Confidential Information to any country to which such export or transmission is restricted by regulation or statute; and (g) promptly provide the Discloser with notice of any actual or threatened breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall: (a) only disclose the Confidential Information to those employees and contractors with a need to know; provided that Recipient binds those employees and contractors to terms as restrictive as those stated in this Agreement (or, with respect to professional advisors, advises them of its obligations with respect to the Confidential Information); (b) disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, without Discloser’s prior written consent; (c) use such Confidential Information only to the extent required to accomplish the Purpose; (d) not reproduce Confidential Information in any form except as required to accomplish the Purpose; (e) not reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any software disclosed by the Discloser; (f) not directly or indirectly export or transmit any Confidential Information to any country to which such export or transmission is restricted by regulation or statute; and (g) promptly provide the Discloser with notice of any actual or threatened breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'not disclose' to 'disclose' in subsection (b), it creates an in-text contradiction. The section now states that the Recipient 'shall disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, without Discloser's prior written consent', directly contradicting the fundamental principle of non-disclosure that underlies the entire agreement. This makes the enforceability of any other confidentiality provisions within the agreement questionable, since it goes against the purpose of this Agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction, which states the opposite of non-disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition. Subject to the limitations set forth in Section 2, all information (including but not limited to ideas, techniques, works of authorship, models, inventions, know-how, processes, equipment, algorithms, software source documents and formulae related to the current, future, and proposed products and services of each of the parties and information concerning research, development, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, customer lists, business forecasts, sales and merchandising, and marketing plans), disclosed by one party (the “Discloser”) to the other party (the “Recipient”) which is labeled or marked “Confidential” or with some other similar proprietary legend, or which, in the context of the disclosure, the Recipient has reason to believe is confidential, shall be deemed to be “Confidential Information.”
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section (b) from 'not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party' to 'disclose any Confidential Information to any third party'. This directly contradicts the fundamental principle of confidentiality and the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the 'Contradicted Text', which implies non-disclosure. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the changed text now enforces disclosure without consent, which is the opposite of maintaining confidentiality, making the enforceability of other provisions questionable.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) shall remain the property of Discloser. All copies shall bear all proprietary markings contained on or in the originals.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) shall remain the property of Recipient. All copies shall bear all proprietary markings contained on or in the originals.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Discloser' to 'Recipient', ownership of Confidential Information is transferred. This now contradicts the definition of a Non-Disclosure Agreement. This also creates issues with Section 3, which talks about the need to protect Confidential Information, yet it is no longer the owner. The in-text contradiction in this case, completely opposes the nature of this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall: (a) only disclose the Confidential Information to those employees and contractors with a need to know; provided that Recipient binds those employees and contractors to terms at least as restrictive as those stated in this Agreement (or, with respect to professional advisors, advises them of its obligations with respect to the Confidential Information); (b) not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, without Discloser’s prior written consent; (c) use such Confidential Information only to the extent required to accomplish the Purpose; (d) not reproduce Confidential Information in any form except as required to accomplish the Purpose; (e) not reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any software disclosed by the Discloser; (f) not directly or indirectly export or transmit any Confidential Information to any country to which such export or transmission is restricted by regulation or statute; and (g) promptly provide the Discloser with notice of any actual or threatened breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text assigns ownership of Confidential Information to the Recipient, which directly contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text imposes obligations on the Recipient to protect the Discloser's Confidential Information. If the Recipient owns the information, these obligations become nonsensical, as one cannot have obligations to protect their own property for the benefit of another party.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the final, complete and exclusive agreement of the parties relative to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings and agreements relating to its subject matter and may not be changed, modified, amended or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the final, complete and exclusive agreement of the parties relative to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings and agreements relating to its subject matter and may be changed, modified, amended or supplemented verbally by either party.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the clause to allow verbal modifications, it creates an in-text contradiction with the original text that requires a 'written instrument signed by both parties' for any changes. The modification undermines the certainty and enforceability of the original agreement, as it opens the door to disputes over verbal agreements. The in-text contradiction, is created when verbally the agreement can be updated even though the text in general speaks about keeping confidence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California. This Agreement contains the final, complete and exclusive agreement of the parties relative to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings and agreements relating to its subject matter and may be changed, modified, amended or supplemented verbally by either party. If any provision of this Agreement is found by a proper authority to be unenforceable, that provision shall be severed and the remainder of this Agreement will continue in full force and effect. Recipient hereby acknowledges and agrees that no remedy at law will afford Discloser adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, breach of Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that Discloser shall be entitled to specific performance of Recipient’s obligations, as well as such further equitable relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that modifications must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text allows verbal modifications by either party, directly contradicting the original requirement. The contradicted text repeats this contradiction. This is a strong, direct contradiction and would cause confusion on how the contract can be modified.
